info.description: Administrative API for managing gateways and their registries, policies, consumers, roles and auth credentials.
info.title: TrustGate Admin API

get.description: Returns build/version information for the running binary.
Reports whether the process is alive.
Reports whether the process is ready to serve traffic.
Returns a paginated list of gateways.
Returns a paginated list of auths in a gateway.

post.description: Creates a new auth in a gateway.

get.description: Returns a single auth by id.

delete.description: Deletes an auth from a gateway.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of consumers in a gateway.

post.description: Creates a new consumer in a gateway.

get.description: Returns a single consumer by id.

delete.description: Deletes a consumer from a gateway.

post.description: Associates an auth credential with a consumer (idempotent).
post.summary: Attach an auth to a consumer

delete.description: Removes the association between an auth credential and a consumer (idempotent).
delete.summary: Detach an auth from a consumer

post.description: Associates a policy with a consumer (idempotent). Editing the policy later affects every consumer it is attached to.
post.summary: Attach a policy to a consumer

delete.description: Removes the association between a policy and a consumer (idempotent).
delete.summary: Detach a policy from a consumer

post.description: Associates a registry with a consumer (idempotent). The optional body sets the registry weight for weighted load balancing.
post.summary: Attach a registry to a consumer

delete.description: Removes the association between a registry and a consumer (idempotent).
delete.summary: Detach a registry from a consumer

post.description: Associates a role with a role_based consumer (idempotent). Returns 409 for inline consumers.
post.summary: Attach a role to a consumer

delete.description: Removes the association between a role and a consumer (idempotent).
delete.summary: Detach a role from a consumer

get.description: Returns a paginated list of policies in a gateway.

post.description: Creates a new policy in a gateway.

get.description: Returns a single policy by id.

delete.description: Deletes a policy from a gateway.

post.description: Creates a copy of an existing policy. The new policy reuses the plugin configuration (slug, settings, stages, enabled, priority, parallel) with a fresh id and an auto-generated name (suffix 2, 3, 4...). The copy has no consumer associations and is not global.
Promotes a policy to gateway-wide scope (applies to every consumer).

delete.description: Demotes a global policy back to consumer-scoped (applies only to linked consumers).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of registries in a gateway.

post.description: Creates a new backend in a gateway.

Validates connectivity and credentials against the provider's API with a lightweight, auth-only request. Test either a stored registry (registry_id) or an inline candidate configuration (provider + auth). Always returns 200; inspect "ok" and "stage" for the outcome.

get.description: Returns a single backend by id.

delete.description: Deletes a backend from a gateway.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of roles in a gateway.

post.description: Creates a new role in a gateway. model_policies cannot be set on create; bind registries first, then update the role.

get.description: Returns a role by id within a gateway.

put.description: Updates a role. model_policies may only reference registries already attached to the role.

delete.description: Deletes a role from a gateway.

post.description: Associates a registry with a role (idempotent).
post.summary: Attach a registry to a role

delete.description: Removes the association between a registry and a role (idempotent).
delete.summary: Detach a registry from a role

get.description: Returns a single gateway by id.

delete.description: Deletes a gateway and cascades the deletion to every resource that belongs to it (consumers, roles, policies, auths, registries and vault credentials).

get.description: Returns the curated catalog of well-known remote MCP servers, used to prefill MCP registry creation.
Returns the catalog of supported models, optionally filtered by provider.
Returns the metrics Event captured for a playground request, keyed by the X-AG-Trace-Id returned in the proxy response. Traces expire after a short TTL.

parameters.description: Trace id (X-AG-Trace-Id from the proxy response)
parameters.name: trace_id

get.description: Returns the catalog of available policies grouped by type. Each entry includes the settings schema needed to render its configuration form dynamically.
Returns the catalog of supported LLM providers.

post.description: Forwards an OpenAI Chat Completions request to the selected provider. Proxy plane route: /{consumer_slug}/v1/chat/completions. Other fixed routes include /v1/messages (Anthropic) and /v1/responses (OpenAI Responses).

parameters.description: Bearer token for OAuth2 or OIDC consumers
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Gateway slug when using header-based gateway discovery
parameters.name: X-AG-Gateway-Slug

weight.description: Weight is the relative weighted-round-robin share on a 1..100 scale.
Weight is the relative weighted-round-robin share on a 1..100 scale.

fields.description: Fields lists the child fields of an object.

item.description: Item describes the element schema of an array.

key_options.description: KeyOptions lists the well-known keys of a map. Empty means free-form keys.

value.description: Value describes the value schema of a map.

scheme.description:
Scheme is the credential prefix the gateway prepends to the supplied
secret when building the header value: Bearer | Token | Basic | ApiKey |
App | raw (raw = send the value verbatim, no prefix).

authorize_url.description:
AuthorizeURL / TokenURL are required for manual registration
(Registration == "manual"), where the operator supplies a pre-registered
client_id/secret; they also serve as a discovery fallback otherwise.

dcr.description:
DCR reports whether the server supports OAuth Dynamic Client Registration
(RFC 7591). A nil pointer means it could not be determined (e.g. a
tenant-templated host that must be probed per-instance).

pkce.description:
PKCE reports whether the authorization server supports PKCE (S256). A nil
pointer means it could not be determined.

registration.description:
Registration is the recommended gateway registration mode derived from
DCR support: "auto" when the server supports Dynamic Client Registration
(the gateway self-registers and the user just logs in at runtime),
"manual" when an operator must pre-register a client. Empty means the
server is tenant-hosted and discovery happens per-instance at connect time.

resource.description: Resource is the RFC 8707 resource indicator / expected token audience.

scopes.description: Scopes are the default/required OAuth scopes for the server.

relevance.description:
Relevance ranks how broadly relevant a server is for enterprises
(higher = more relevant). Used to sort the catalog; 0 means unranked.

requires_config.description:
RequiresConfig reports whether the operator must supply input before the
server can be connected (a required URL variable, a static secret, or a
manual/tenant OAuth client). When false the UI can connect it by default
without a configuration step: public servers, and OAuth servers whose
client self-registers (registration "auto") where the user simply logs in
at runtime.

tools.description:
Tools is a snapshot of the server's advertised tools, captured by an
unauthenticated tools/list where the server allows it. It is a preview for
the catalog UI; the authoritative per-connection tool set is discovered at
runtime by the gateway's introspector (and may be tenant/user-specific).
Empty when the server requires auth to list tools.

in.description: In is where the variable is substituted: "path" (default) or "query".

secret.description:
Secret marks a variable that carries a credential (e.g. a token passed in
the query string) so the UI/secret store treats it as sensitive.
